message.content:
Research Kiera Hamilton and find multiple key decision makers at JLW Fitout (an Australian fitout/interiors company). The company has info@jlwinteriors.com.au and 1300 559 477 as company-level contacts.

I need:
1. Kiera Hamilton's direct contact (personal email, mobile, LinkedIn)
2. Other key decision makers at JLW Fitout (directors, owners, operations managers, procurement)
3. For each person found: name, title, email, phone, LinkedIn URL

Search the web for:
- "Kiera Hamilton" "JLW" OR "JLW Fitout" email LinkedIn
- "JLW Fitout" OR "JLW Interiors" team directors leadership
- site:linkedin.com "JLW Fitout" OR "JLW Interiors"
- "JLW Interiors" about team management

The goal is to find MULTIPLE contacts at this company, not just one. Report ALL findings with source URLs.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init
- review
- security-review

content.text:
I have all the data available in the project. Unfortunately, all web access tools (WebSearch, WebFetch, Bash/curl) are denied in this session, so I cannot perform live web research. Here is what I found from existing project data, along with the specific searches you should run manually.

---

## Existing Data: JLW Fitout (LEAD-0133)

**Company-level contacts already recorded:**
- Company email: `info@jlwinteriors.com.au`
- Company phone: `1300 559 477`
- Contact form: `https://www.jlwinteriors.com.au/contact/`
- Website: `https://www.jlwinteriors.com.au/`
- Team page (source): `https://www.jlwinteriors.com.au/our-team/`

**Key people already identified (from contacts.csv, all sourced from the official team page):**

| # | Name | Title | Department | Role Type | Contact ID |
|---|------|-------|------------|-----------|------------|
| 1 | **John Williams** | Founder & Director | Management | Decision Maker | CONTACT-0019 |
| 2 | **Jeremy Wearne** | Senior Project Manager | Project Delivery | Project Decision Influencer | CONTACT-0020 |
| 3 | **Joanna Renny** | Finance & Administration Manager | Finance / Administration | Supplier/Admin Contact | CONTACT-0021 |
| 4 | **Kiera Hamilton** | Head of Business Development & Strategy | Business Development | Decision Maker (primary) | CONTACT-0022 |

**All four contacts are missing personal email, phone, and LinkedIn URLs.** They were identified from the official team page only, with "company_route" level contact (score 60 in KP pipeline). The pipeline assigned Kiera Hamilton `info@jlwinteriors.com.au` and `1300 559 477` as fallback company contacts, not direct personal ones.

---

## What You Need to Search Manually

Since I cannot access the web, here are the exact searches to run:

### For Kiera Hamilton specifically:
1. `"Kiera Hamilton" "JLW" email` -- look for personal email patterns
2. `site:linkedin.com "Kiera Hamilton" "JLW"` -- LinkedIn profile
3. `"Kiera Hamilton" "JLW Interiors" OR "JLW Fitout" LinkedIn` -- broader LinkedIn search
4. `site:au.linkedin.com/in/ "kiera hamilton"` -- direct LinkedIn URL guess

### For other JLW decision makers:
5. `site:linkedin.com "John Williams" "JLW"` -- Founder/Director LinkedIn
6. `site:linkedin.com "Jeremy Wearne" "JLW"` -- Senior PM LinkedIn
7. `site:linkedin.com "Joanna Renny" "JLW"` -- Finance Manager LinkedIn
8. `"JLW Interiors" OR "JLW Fitout" director owner management` -- find any additional leaders not on the team page

### Email pattern exploration:
JLW uses `@jlwinteriors.com.au`. Common patterns to test:
- `kiera.hamilton@jlwinteriors.com.au`
- `kiera@jlwinteriors.com.au`
- `khamilton@jlwinteriors.com.au`
- `john.williams@jlwinteriors.com.au`
- `john@jlwinteriors.com.au`
- `jeremy.wearne@jlwinteriors.com.au`
- `joanna.renny@jlwinteriors.com.au`

---

## Priority Assessment

**Kiera Hamilton** is the best outreach target -- her title "Head of Business Development & Strategy" means she handles partnerships and supplier relationships. She is marked as primary contact (is_primary_contact=Yes).

**John Williams** (Founder & Director) is the ultimate decision maker but may be harder to reach directly.

**Joanna Renny** (Finance & Admin) is relevant for procurement/payments once a relationship is established.

**Jeremy Wearne** (Senior PM) is relevant for project-level discussions.

---

## Recommended Next Steps

1. Open a browser and run the LinkedIn searches above to find personal LinkedIn URLs for all four people.
2. If LinkedIn profiles are found, check for email patterns visible on the profile.
3. Use Hunter.io or similar email verification tools with domain `jlwinteriors.com.au` to discover email format.
4. Once direct contacts are found, update `data/contacts.csv` with the personal email/phone/LinkedIn for each contact.

**Bottom line:** The project already has 4 JLW team members identified from their official /our-team/ page, but none have direct personal contact details (email, phone, LinkedIn). All four need enrichment via LinkedIn search and email pattern testing. I was unable to perform live web research due to tool restrictions -- the searches listed above need to be run manually.
